---
title: "Posit AI Setup"
subtitle: "Use Posit Assistant in RStudio, Positron, or VS Code"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

# Using Posit AI

This page explains how to start using [Posit AI](https://posit.ai/) for this course.

Posit AI provides access to Posit Assistant. It works with RStudio and is compatible with Positron and VS Code-style editor workflows. 

The official getting-started guide is here:

<https://docs.posit.co/posit-ai/user/getting-started/>

At the time of course preparation, Posit AI advertises a free trial credit. Check <https://posit.ai/> for the current trial amount and billing details. This trial should get you through this course.

# When To Use This Option

Posit AI is a good option if you want an AI assistant integrated with Posit tools rather than a separate command-line agent.

Use it if:

- you use RStudio and want the assistant inside RStudio,
- you use Positron and want the assistant inside the editor,
- you want a course workflow that stays close to R and Quarto.

Posit AI also has the benefit of a zero data retention agreement with Anthropic, which provides the models backing the service.


# RStudio Setup

Follow the official Posit AI RStudio instructions:

<https://docs.posit.co/posit-ai/user/getting-started/#rstudio>

The basic workflow is:

1. Install the current version of RStudio Desktop.
2. Open RStudio.
3. Show Posit Assistant from the toolbar icon in the upper-right area of the IDE.
4. If prompted, install Posit AI.
5. Verify that you trust the workspace.
6. Click **Sign In**.
7. Authorize in the browser and return to RStudio.
8. Confirm that Posit Assistant is ready.

# Positron Or VS Code Setup

Follow the official Posit AI Positron instructions:

<https://docs.posit.co/posit-ai/user/getting-started/#positron>

The basic workflow is:

1. Install the current version of Positron.
2. Open Positron.
3. Open Posit Assistant from the activity bar.
4. Click **Help me set it up**.
5. Click **Enable Posit Assistant**.
6. Configure language model providers.
7. Choose **Posit AI** and click **Sign In**.
8. Authorize in the browser and return to Positron.
9. Confirm that Posit Assistant is ready.

If you use VS Code, follow the closest matching Posit Assistant or extension instructions available for your setup. The important course workflow is the same: open only the assigned copied work folder and keep prompts outside that folder unless instructed otherwise.

# Course Workflow Reminder

For course tasks, do not open the full course repository as the agent context unless the task explicitly asks for it. Do not let agents edit course-owned folders such as `modules/` or `practice/tasks/`; your editable notebooks belong in `practice/work/`.

For Day 2, open:

```text
practice/work/01_clean-and-visualize/
```

Then use the prompt from:

```text
practice/prompts/01_clean-and-visualize/prompt.txt
```

Paste the prompt into Posit Assistant. Do not copy the prompt file into the work folder.

# What To Check

Before relying on Posit Assistant, confirm:

- you are signed in to Posit AI,
- the assigned folder under `practice/work/` is open,
- the file explorer does not include unrelated course folders,
- code runs locally,
- rendered output uses the local `data/` folder,
- you manually check generated tables, plots, and interpretations.

# If Something Fails

Check the official Posit AI documentation first:

<https://docs.posit.co/posit-ai/user/getting-started/>

Bring the exact error message to class if setup still fails.
